data.current_description: Creates a new version for the specified site.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new version for a site
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Version for the specified site. A Version is a configuration and collection of static files that determine how the site is displayed. Requires a Version object in the request body specifying the configuration. Returns the created Version object with assigned identifiers, creation metadata, and status information. The version is initially in a draft state and must be populated with files and finalized before it can be released to a channel. Use this operation to prepare new site configurations for deployment.

data.current_description: Creates a new version on the specified target site using the content of the specified version.
data.summary_suggestion: Clone a version to another site
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Version on the specified target site by copying the content and configuration from a source version. Requires a CloneVersionRequest object specifying the source version and target site. Returns the newly created Version object on the target site with the cloned content. The cloned version is in draft state and can be modified independently from the source. This operation is useful for replicating site configurations across multiple sites or creating version backups. The source version's files and configuration are duplicated; the clone is not linked to the original.
data.reasoning: Original had no summary and incomplete description. Added summary for clarity. data.current_description: Adds content files to the specified version. Each file must be under 2 GB.
data.summary_suggestion: Add content files to a version
data.description_suggestion: Adds content files to the specified Version. Requires a PopulateVersionFilesRequest object containing the files to upload. Each individual file must not exceed 2 GB in size. Returns a response confirming the files have been added to the version. After populating files, the version must be finalized before it can be released to a channel. This operation is part of the version deployment workflow where you first create a version, populate it with files, then finalize and release it. Use this to upload your site's static content, assets, and configurations.
